old_claim.note: Writing is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim.note: The scoped qualification is awarded by a university after an organized course of study.

source_shape.source_locator: Entry: says academic degrees originated in medieval European universities; the first known degree was awarded to civil-law doctoral candidates at Bologna in the twelfth century.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The first known academic degree was awarded at the medieval University of Bologna in the twelfth century.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The replacement follows the mechanism documented for the scoped milestone. A university degree requires an awarding university and course of study, while writing alone is only generic background infrastructure.

ontology_before: university_degrees depended on the broad or mismatched prerequisite writing.
ontology_after: university_degrees now depends on universities_research_curricula, which matches the directly documented mechanism.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: university_degrees depends on universities_research_curricula and no longer on writing.
would_reject_if: A reliable source shows writing is indispensable while universities_research_curricula is absent from the current university_degrees scope.
short_reason: A university degree requires an awarding university and course of study, while writing alone is only generic background infrastructure.
